properties.name: Talk4Now
properties.description:
🌍 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗧𝗮𝗹𝗸𝟰𝗡𝗼𝘄 — 𝗧𝗮𝗹𝗸 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱, 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗙𝗿𝗲𝗲

Learning a language shouldn’t be lonely.
It shouldn’t be expensive.
And it definitely shouldn’t be boring.

That’s why we built Talk4Now — a global platform where you can practice any language by talking to real people from around the world 💬🌎

On Talk4Now, you don’t just study a language.
You use it.

You can create or join rooms labeled by language, meet learners and native speakers, and talk with multiple people at the same time. Whether you’re learning English, Spanish, Arabic, French, or any other language, there’s a place for you here.

✨ 𝑾𝒉𝒂𝒕 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒄𝒂𝒏 𝒅𝒐 𝒐𝒏 𝑻𝒂𝒍𝒌4𝑵𝒐𝒘:
• Create language rooms and set your own topics
• Talk with multiple people in real time
• Follow users, make friends, and stay connected
• Message your friends using Hangouts
• Share your screen, watch YouTube together, and use room tools like:
 – 📋 Room To-Do List
 – ⏱ Pomodoro Timer
 – 💬 Room Chat with emojis & photos

Room creators become the Owner, with control to set titles, topics, limits, and even assign Co-Owners. We also protect our community with strong rules — anyone who breaks them can be reported and removed to keep Talk4Now safe and welcoming for everyone 🤝

🏆 𝗪𝗲 𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝗮 𝗟𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿𝗯𝗼𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝘄𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝘀𝗲𝗲 𝘄𝗵𝗼’𝘀 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺!

And the best part?

🆓 Talk4Now is completely free for everyone.
No time limits, No paywalls, Just people talking to people.

For those who want to support the platform and unlock extras, we also offer premium tiers like Ambassador, VIP, and Elite with special badges, visibility boosts, customization, and early access to new tools.

🌐 𝗪𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗲𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗴𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱.
And Talk4Now exists to bring people closer — one conversation at a time.

results.title: Conversation Exchange
results.author: Exa
highlights:
Conversation Exchange is a E-learning company. Conversation Exchange is a language learning platform that offers three types of language exchange: face to face conversation, correspondence, text and voice chat. Conversation Exchange employs 2 people, founded in 2005. Headquartered in San Antonio, Texas, United States, with presence in Italy and Brazil.
[...]
Through Conversation Exchange you can practice your second language with native speakers living in your area.
[...]
Pedro is from Spain and has recently moved to New York. His English is a bit patchy and he would like to improve it. John is from New York and he is learning Spanish, but he never has the opportunity to listen and speak to native Spanish speakers. Now, John and Pedro could help each other out. For example they can meet up in a café once a week and have a conversation exchange, speaking half an hour in English and half an hour in Spanish. There is no cost or homework involved and you learn the language in a social environment. Furthermore, you are more likely to learn slang and other things about a culture that you do not usually learn in a formal language class.
[...]
- Tech Stack: skype, whatsapp
[...]
language learning, language exchange community, face to face conversation, language exchange, native speakers, text and voice chat, correspondence
[...]
- [2025-08-02] We integrated audio/video to ChitChat (desktop/laptop at the moment) so you can have calls with your language partners without disclosing your phone or other app IDs to strangers right off the bat. (4 reactions, 2 comments)

properties.name: Conversation Exchange
properties.description:
Through Conversation Exchange you can practice your second language with native speakers living in your area.

But how exactly does it work?

Well, the best way to explain it is through an example.

Pedro is from Spain and has recently moved to New York. His English is a bit patchy and he would like to improve it. John is from New York and he is learning Spanish, but he never has the opportunity to listen and speak to native Spanish speakers. Now, John and Pedro could help each other out. For example they can meet up in a café once a week and have a conversation exchange, speaking half an hour in English and half an hour in Spanish. There is no cost or homework involved and you learn the language in a social environment. Furthermore, you are more likely to learn slang and other things about a culture that you do not usually learn in a formal language class.

results.title: HelloTalk
results.author: Exa
highlights:
HelloTalk, also abbreviated as HT, is a language exchange app and website released in 2013. It is monetized using the freemium model. It was created by Chinese developer Zackery Ngai. As of December 2025, the platform supports learning and communication in more than 260 languages, including English, Japanese, Spanish, French, Chinese, Korean, and German. results.title: PenPal
results.author: Exa
highlights:
PenPal is a Social Networking Platforms company. PenPal is a platform that allows users to sign up and match with other members based on mutual interests, chat and create customized postcards online. The platform is simple and fun, with features such as creating personalized postcards and hidden addresses. Users can learn about other cultures and countries through the platform. PenPal employs 3 people, founded in 2022. Headquartered in Berlin, Berlin, Germany, with presence in Spain and Philippines. Has $149.3K in total funding.
[...]
There's a global social media fatigue, and connections online feel superficial, which leaves many people lonely. That's why we created PenPal, a social matching platform for building meaningful relationships with a physical touch. Members can chat, and create postcards digitally, that are then sent physically via mail without ever sharing their address publicly.
[...]
PenPal is a platform for thoughtful communication, where people can connect and share exciting stories and passions with new friends to build lasting relationships. To achieve that, we bridge the gap between digital and physical communication.
[...]
You match with like-minded members from across the globe and communicate through personalized postcards and direct messages with each other. Writing a real card fosters genuine communication, and people can slow down from the daily pace of life.
[...]
Our members are part of an open and safe community where everyone is welcome – and can be themselves. You can learn about languages, countries, cultures, and traditions. We hope to contribute with that to a more inclusive, tolerant, and understanding society on a global scale.
[...]

properties.name: PenPal
properties.description:
There's a global social media fatigue, and connections online feel superficial, which leaves many people lonely. That's why we created PenPal, a social matching platform for building meaningful relationships with a physical touch. Members can chat, and create postcards digitally, that are then sent physically via mail without ever sharing their address publicly.

PenPal is a platform for thoughtful communication, where people can connect and share exciting stories and passions with new friends to build lasting relationships. To achieve that, we bridge the gap between digital and physical communication.

You match with like-minded members from across the globe and communicate through personalized postcards and direct messages with each other. Writing a real card fosters genuine communication, and people can slow down from the daily pace of life.

Our members are part of an open and safe community where everyone is welcome – and can be themselves. You can learn about languages, countries, cultures, and traditions. We hope to contribute with that to a more inclusive, tolerant, and understanding society on a global scale.

results.title: FluentPal AB
results.author: Exa
highlights:
FluentPal AB is a Education company. FluentPal is a language learning platform that allows users to talk to a language and be matched to a language partner. It offers instant matching experience and personalized language practice. The app matches users with compatible language partners based on their preferences, making language exchange effortless and efficient. It also features dynamic conversations, quizzes, and feedback, as well as personalized avatars. FluentPal AB employs 2 people, founded in 2026. Headquartered in Linköping, Sweden, with presence in Switzerland.
[...]
FluentPal connects language learners with real practice partners — people at your level who want to speak, not just swipe through flashcards. Think of it as the next step: where vocabulary becomes conversation and practice becomes friendship.
[...]
Our matching system pairs learners who share a language goal. Both people show up. Both people grow. No tutors, no bots, no streaks — just two people helping each other get fluent.
[...]
Whether you're preparing for a move abroad, keeping a language alive, or finally ready to speak after months of solo study — FluentPal is where you find your person.

properties.name: FluentPal AB
properties.description:
You finished Duolingo. Now what?
FluentPal connects language learners with real practice partners — people at your level who want to speak, not just swipe through flashcards. Think of it as the next step: where vocabulary becomes conversation and practice becomes friendship.
Our matching system pairs learners who share a language goal. Both people show up. Both people grow. No tutors, no bots, no streaks — just two people helping each other get fluent.
Whether you're preparing for a move abroad, keeping a language alive, or finally ready to speak after months of solo study — FluentPal is where you find your person.
Currently launching with Spanish and English. More languages coming soon.

highlights:

[...]

, +10 people), founded in 2017. Headquartered in Washington, District of Columbia, United States, operates in 13 countries (including Mexico
[...]
Peru, and Benin).
[...]
Conversifi connects foreign language learners with native speakers for on-demand language practice and cross-cultural exchange over video chat. 

We believe immersion, alongside formal instruction, is the best way to master another language and learn about another culture. Conversations on Conversifi are based on customized immersion modules that track, complement and reinforce learners’ offline or online course curricula. Language students and faculty alike are lauding our cutting-edge approach.

properties.name: Conversifi
properties.description:
Conversifi connects foreign language learners with native speakers for on-demand language practice and cross-cultural exchange over video chat. 

We believe immersion, alongside formal instruction, is the best way to master another language and learn about another culture. Conversations on Conversifi are based on customized immersion modules that track, complement and reinforce learners’ offline or online course curricula. Language students and faculty alike are lauding our cutting-edge approach. 

We’re on a mission to make language immersion and exchange accessible to everyone on a consistent basis in order to increase cross-cultural understanding and make the world a less violent and more innovative place.

results.title: Best Language Exchange Apps in 2026: What Actually Works
highlights:
### 1. SewaYou
[...]
SewaYou is not a language exchange app in the traditional sense. It is a map-based friendship app. You open it and see people on a map. Tap once to see who is near you in your neighborhood. Tap again to zoom out to the entire globe and find people anywhere in the world. Language practice happens naturally because you are meeting people who speak different languages. No artificial "you teach me, I teach you" structure.
[...]
Why it works: The best language exchange does not feel like language exchange. It feels like hanging out with a friend who happens to speak another language. SewaYou is built around that idea. Find someone two stations away and grab coffee, or connect with someone across the world and chat. Your language improves because the conversation is real, not a drill.
[...]
- Map-based discovery: zoom into your neighborhood or zoom out to the whole world
- Built for real-world meetups when people are nearby, seamless chat when they are not
- Friendship-first approach means conversations feel natural, not transactional
- Active community in Japanese cities and growing globally

with video/voice calls

results.title: HelloTalk vs Tandem: Which Language Exchange App Is Better in 2026?
highlights:
An honest side-by-side comparison of HelloTalk and Tandem after months of real use. Plus, what both apps are missing.
[...]
If you have Googled "best language exchange app," you have seen
[...]
same two names everywhere: HelloTalk and Tandem. They look similar. They promise similar things. And choosing between them is genuinely confusing.
[...]
HelloTalk is built around text-based learning. It has a social feed (Moments), translation tools, and correction features baked into the chat. Think of it as a language learning tool with a social layer.
[...]
Tandem is built around speaking. It has video calls, voice notes, and a tutor marketplace. Think of it as a conversation-first app with learning tools on the side.
[...]
Both let you find partners by language pair and send messages for free. The difference is in what they optimize for.
[...]
HelloTalk wins. For most language pairs, HelloTalk simply has more users. Search for Japanese speakers learning English and you will find hundreds of active profiles. On Tandem, the same search returns fewer results, especially outside major languages.
[...]
For less common languages (Portuguese, Turkish, Vietnamese), the gap is even wider. HelloTalk has coverage. Tandem has gaps.
[...]
One exception: Tandem seems to attract slightly older, more serious learners. If you are in your late 20s or 30s and want partners in the same age range, Tandem may feel more comfortable.
[...]
HelloTalk conversations tend to follow a pattern: "Hi, how are you?" followed by basic Q&A, followed by silence. The correction tools are great when people use them, but many conversations never get deep enough for meaningful corrections. The Moments feed helps break the ice, but it can also feel like a distraction.
[...]
Tandem conversations start awkwardly (video calling a stranger is inherently awkward) but tend to be more substantive once you push through the initial discomfort. The video format forces both people to actually engage instead of half-heartedly texting.
[...]
Verdict: Tandem produces better individual conversations. HelloTalk produces more conversations overall. Pick your priority.
[...]
Both apps have the same fundamental problem: people disappear.
[...]
You spend 20 minutes writing a thoughtful introduction. The other person reads it and never replies. Or you have three great exchanges and then they vanish. This is not unique to either app. It is the
[...]
of free language exchange.
[...]
In our experience, the flake rate on both apps is roughly the same: about 70% of initial conversations go nowhere. You need to message 10 people to find 3 who will respond consistently.
[...]
HelloTalk Free:
[...]
- Unlimited text messages
- One language at a time
- Translation and correction tools
- Moments feed
- Ads
[...]
HelloTalk VIP ($7-12/month):
[...]
- Multiple languages
- Advanced translation
- No ads
- See who viewed your profile
[...]
Tandem Free:
[...]
- Limited messages per day
- One language at a time
- Basic translation
[...]
Tandem Pro ($7-15/month):
[...]
- Unlimited messages
- Multiple languages
- See who is nearby
- Advanced filters
[...]
Verdict: HelloTalk is more generous with free features. Tandem's free tier feels restrictive and pushes you toward paying.
[...]
After months of use, the biggest frustration with both HelloTalk and Tandem is the same: everything stays online.
[...]
You text. You maybe video call. But you never actually meet anyone. And research consistently shows that language learning accelerates dramatically when you practice in person, face to face, with all the social pressure and body language and spontaneous moments that screens cannot replicate.
[...]
Neither HelloTalk nor Tandem has a "find someone near me" feature that is designed around meeting up. They are global chat platforms. Great for connecting with someone in Brazil while you sit in Tokyo. Less great for finding a conversation partner you can grab coffee with this Saturday.
[...]
This is exactly the gap SewaYou fills. It is a map-based friendship app where you see people on a map, whether they are in your neighborhood or on the other side of the world. When someone is nearby, you meet up. When they are far, you chat. Language exchange happens naturally because you are having real conversations with real people, not drilling each other through a screen.
[...]
If you are tired of online-only exchanges that never leave the chat window, try SewaYou. It is not a replacement for HelloTalk or Tandem. It is the missing piece: the part where you actually meet someone.
[...]
Choose HelloTalk if:
[...]
- You want the largest user base
- Text-based learning is your priority
- You want strong free features
- You enjoy social feed content
[...]
Choose Tandem if:
[...]
- You prefer speaking over texting
- You want slightly more serious partners
- You are willing to pay for the full experience
- Video calls do not intimidate you
[...]
Choose SewaYou if:
[...]
- You want to meet people nearby or discover friends around the globe
- You learn better through genuine conversation than structured drills
- You want a map view that shows you real people, not just a list of profiles
- You are tired of online-only exchanges that never leave the chat
[...]
Is HelloTalk or Tandem better for Japanese? HelloTalk has more Japanese users overall. But if you are physically in Japan, SewaYou connects you with Japanese speakers nearby for in-person practice, which is more effective than either.
[...]
Can I use HelloTalk and Tandem together? Yes, and many people do. Use HelloTalk for daily text practice and Tandem for weekly speaking sessions. Add SewaYou for in-person meetups to complete the triangle.
[...]
Are HelloTalk and Tandem free? Both have free tiers. HelloTalk's free version is more usable. Tandem's free tier limits daily messages, which can be frustrating.
[...]
Which app has less spam? Tandem's verification process reduces spam slightly. But both apps have reports of unwanted messages, especially from users looking for dates instead of language exchange. Report and block features exist on both platforms.
[...]
What about italki? italki is a paid tutoring marketplace, not a free language exchange. It is excellent for structured lessons with professional teachers, but it is a different product entirely. You can use it alongside HelloTalk/Tandem/SewaYou without overlap.

results.title: The 12 Best Language Exchange Apps for Fluent Conversations in 2026 | Translate AI Blog | Translate AI
results.author: Translate AI Team
highlights:
## 1. Tandem
[...]
Tandem secures the top spot as one of the best language exchange apps by creating a vibrant, peer-to-peer community focused on authentic conversation. It connects millions of users with native speakers across more than 300 languages, including several forms of Sign Language (ASL). The platform's strength lies in its comprehensive toolset designed to facilitate real learning within a chat interface.
[...]
Unlike simple chat apps, Tandem integrates features like in-line translation, message correction, and voice notes directly into the conversation. This allows your partner to correct your mistakes in real-time without disrupting the flow. The transition from text to audio or video calls is seamless, offering a natural progression as your confidence grows. These features are particularly useful for enhancing your real-time conversation skills, a key component of language mastery. For more strategies on this, you can explore tips to improve listening comprehension.
[...]
## 2. HelloTalk
[...]
HelloTalk stands out as one of the best language exchange apps by transforming language practice into a multifaceted social experience. It supports over 260 languages and connects a massive, active community of learners and native speakers. The app moves beyond simple one-on-one chats, offering dynamic group "Voicerooms" and livestreams where users can drop in to listen or participate in real-time conversations, creating an immersive and community-driven learning environment.
[...]
Its strength lies in the powerful AI-aided learning tools integrated directly into the chat. Features like instant translation, transliteration for different scripts, and text-to-speech help learners understand and pronounce new words correctly. The social media-style "Moments" feed allows users to share updates with the entire community, getting corrections and feedback from multiple native speakers at once. This public practice can significantly boost your conversational skills by exposing you to diverse feedback.
[...]
HelloTalk's interface feels like a blend of a messaging app and a social network, which can be engaging but potentially distracting for users seeking a more focused experience. The free version is quite generous, but a VIP subscription (starting around $12.99/month) is needed to unlock unlimited translations and access more advanced features. The platform’s large user base means finding a partner is almost always instantaneous.
[...]
## 3. Speaky
[...]
Speaky positions itself as a social network for language learners, emphasizing quick connections and straightforward practice. It strips back some of the more complex features of other platforms to focus on the core exchange: connecting you with partners in over 180 countries and 110 languages. Its design prioritizes immediacy, allowing users to quickly find online partners and start a text or audio chat without a lengthy setup process, making it one of the more accessible language exchange apps.
[...]
Speaky’s primary appeal is its simplicity and the fact that its core functionality is completely free. The user interface is clean on both web and mobile, focusing on the chat experience. However, community moderation is less stringent compared to platforms like Tandem, so users may encounter a wider range of partner intentions. A premium subscription is available, which removes ads and offers an unlimited number of translations.
[...]
## 4. Lingbe
[...]
Lingbe carves out its niche among the best language exchange apps with a unique "on-demand" approach to conversation. Instead of scheduling calls or scrolling through profiles, you simply tap a button to be instantly connected with a native speaker for a spontaneous voice chat. This audio-first model is designed to eliminate hesitation and get users speaking immediately, focusing entirely on real-time conversational practice.
[...]
The platform operates on an innovative in-app economy. Users earn currency ("Lingos") by helping others learn their native language, which they can then spend on practice calls for their target language. This creates a reciprocal and gamified system that encourages active participation. The focus remains squarely on speaking and listening, making it an excellent tool for learners who need to overcome the fear of speaking aloud and build conversational fluency without the pressure of video or text.
[...]
Lingbe’s interface is minimalist and purpose-built for its core function: connecting users for voice calls. The user experience is straightforward, though the reliance on earning or purchasing Lingos for extended talk time might not appeal to everyone. Premium plans (starting around $5/month) offer more talking time and features. The app successfully lowers the barrier to entry for speaking practice, making it feel more like a casual phone call than a formal lesson.
[...]
### Core Features & User
[...]
## 6. Hilokal
[...]
Hilokal offers a unique, social-audio approach to language exchange, positioning itself as a platform for live, drop-in conversations. Instead of one-on-one text chats, it centers around audio "tables" or "cafes" where users can gather to discuss specific topics or simply practice speaking. This model lowers the barrier to entry, allowing learners to join rooms as listeners first, absorbing the conversation before feeling pressured to participate.
[...]
The platform is particularly popular for certain language pairings, such as Korean and English, fostering large and active communities. This group-based learning environment is excellent for overcoming speaking anxiety and improving listening skills in a more natural, "party line" style setting. The mobile app further enhances the experience with integrated AI tutoring features, providing a modern supplement to its community-driven core. While a fantastic tool for speaking, users seeking a robust live translation tool for immediate communication needs might find dedicated apps more suitable.
[...]
Hilokal's interface is designed around finding and joining live audio rooms, which are organized by language, topic, and level. The user experience is straightforward, though the web version is more limited compared to the feature-rich mobile app. The free version provides ample access to the core experience, but a premium subscription unlocks features like creating private tables and accessing advanced AI tools. The quality of conversation can vary significantly depending on the room host and active participants at any given time.
[...]
| Tandem | P2P text/voice/video, corrections, 300+ langs | 👥 Conversation-focused learners | ★★★★ | 🏆 Strong moderation & conversation tools ✨ | 💰 Freemium (some paid filters/features) |
[...]
| HelloTalk | 1:1 chat, Voicerooms, livestreams, TTS/AI aids | 👥 Social learners wanting varied practice | ★★★★ | 🏆 Large active community + live events ✨ | 💰 Freemium (VIP subscription) |
[...]
| Speaky | Quick partner discovery, text/voice/calls (170+ langs) | 👥 Casual/free-first learners | ★★★ | ✨ Easy onboarding & free core messaging | 💰 Free-first |
[...]
| Lingbe | Instant tap-to-talk voice calls, in-app rewards | 👥 Learners prioritizing spontaneous speaking | ★★★★ | 🏆 Instant real-time speaking; audio-first ✨ | 💰 Earn/buy minutes (freemium) |
[...]
| Hilokal | Live audio "cafés", community rooms, AI tutor | 👥 Listeners who want low-pressure group practice | ★★★ | ✨ Join-as-listener rooms + AI tutor | 💰 Freemium / in-app features |
[...]
- For the Social Learner: If you want to chat and make friends, choose Tandem or HelloTalk. Your first step: Create a profile and send five personalized messages to partners with similar interests.
[...]
- For the Speaking-Focused: If you just want to practice talking, try Lingbe or Hilokal. Your actionable task: Join one live audio room or make one call this week. Don't overthink it; just press the button.

results.title: HelloTalk - Learn Languages - Apps on Google Play
highlights:
Practice languages with native speakers on HelloTalk! We recently introduced HelloTalk Live and Voiceroom - interactive language and culture events to help you master languages through the content you love! HelloTalk, the original language exchange app, connects you with native speakers to practice languages (English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Mandarin Chinese, Portuguese, German, Italian, Russian, Arabic, Turkish, Hindi, Indonesian, Thai, Vietnamese and 260+ more) for FREE! HelloTalk equips you with learning tools such as translation and instant captions so you can learn by chatting with native speakers. Learn languages from locals around the world through livestreams and Voicerooms. Connect with expert hosts to discuss interesting topics with language partners in the learning community! Why HelloTalk? ► Real language learning atmosphere Whether you're focused on a widely spoken language or a less common one, HelloTalk caters to your needs. The system smartly matches you based on your native language and proficiency, while allowing you to filter ideal language partners according to various criteria such as age, location, and gender. Plus, with built-in translation and transliteration tools, you can communicate confidently without any language barriers.► A powerful tool for sharpening your language skillsAcquiring new words through conversations with native speakers is more effective than rigid memorization. Whether you're a student preparing for exams like the CET, GRE, TOEFL, IELTS, planning to study abroad, or a professional seeking career advancement through language skills, HelloTalk offers native speakers who match your proficiency level for engaging interactions and valuable learning experiences.► Immersive multi-person language chat roomsIf you choose to learn Spanish, for example, every Voiceroom you enter on HelloTalk gathers Spanish learners and Spanish native speakers from around the world to discuss interesting topics. Here, only your voice needs to be heard, creating a comfortable, introvert-friendly atmosphere. Here, sharing mutual interests allows everyone to freely express themselves and share personal experiences, fostering both language intuition and authentic expression skills. It's incredibly addictive!► Diverse global LivestreamsHelloTalk features handpicked foreign language hosts who offer online video lessons, share language insights, and provide glimpses into their lives abroad. You can even join them on stage for one-on-one video conversations with fellow language learners. Whether you're grappling with pronunciation, speaking speed, or shyness, these hosts will assist you in refining your language skills and guiding the conversation. results.title: 96%的自然增长，在大厂没做成的赛道，这家创业公司悄悄做到6000万用户｜对话一线_腾讯新闻
results.author: 腾讯网
highlights:
苏菲是 HelloTalk 的 CMO（首席营销官）。HelloTalk 成立于 2012 年，如今已是全球最大的语言交换学习平台，覆盖 200+ 国家和地区，拥有数千万用户。谈起团队如何在这个被大厂“忽视”的赛道里默默成长，苏菲带着“低调但骄傲”的神采。
[...]
苏菲提到的创始人是魏立华。公开资料显示，这位海南出生的“语言极客”曾辗转全球各地学习和工作，先后学习了英语、日语和韩语等多门语言，最终把“和当地人交朋友学语言”的经验变成了产品。今天的 HelloTalk，用户可以通过聊天、动态、语聊房、直播等方式，与全球语伴建立真实连接。
[...]
谈到如何从 Idea 做到全球 Top1，苏菲的回答是“自然增长”——听起来颇有些“凡尔赛”。这几年 HelloTalk 用户增长很稳也很快，但其中 96% 来自自然流量，在“烧钱换增长”的互联网行业堪称异类。核心用户主要在 18~40 岁，都是高教育水平的“社牛”，但苏菲笑称：“他们不是天生外向，只是 HelloTalk 给了他们‘安全犯错’的空间。”
[...]
AI 正在消弭语言障碍，而 HelloTalk 的解法是“AI 辅助真人”。早在 2013 年，团队就上线了“长按翻译”功能；2017 年自研多语言模型提升准确率；如今 AI 已渗透匹配、纠错、识别等很多环节。苏菲还分享了一个有趣洞察：日语、韩语和中文是平台上最受欢迎的非英语语种，而日韩用户的付费意愿甚至超过美国用户，韩国用户更是高居榜首。
[...]
苏菲：HelloTalk 的品牌定位是“全球知名的跨文化跨语言文化社交平台”，并逐步走向“AI 时代的全球社交网络”。过去，很多人会理解 HelloTalk 是个学外语的工具，尽管我们也确实在语言交换学习这个赛道上有着全球第一的行业地位，但现在我们更希望放大用户对我们“社交”属性的认知——语言是桥梁，是途径；而跨文化连接，交流和沟通才是目的。
[...]
苏菲：其实核心用户一直没变：一群对世界充满好奇的“探索者”。他们表面可能是国际社牛，内核却是“想认真跨文化交流的人”。年龄 18-45 岁，主力在 35 岁以下，高等教育背景居多。
[...]
这类用户的特点是“自驱力强”。他们不满足于被动学习，而是主动寻找真实语境。比如，日本用户喜欢通过动态分享生活，韩国用户热衷语聊房练习口语，而欧洲用户更倾向在讨论跨文化话题中建立深度连接等等。
[...]
我们设计产品时特别注重“安全感”。比如，AI 辅助的实时翻译和语法纠错功能，能帮用户减少表达焦虑；动态圈的匿名反馈机制，让初学者敢开口。数据显示，超 60% 的东亚用户首次交流时选择文字而非语音，但使用 3 个月后，语音互动率会提升至 45%。这说明，只要环境友好，任何人都有可能成为“跨文化社交达人”。
[...]
苏菲：我们从 2012 年就在打磨基础架构，但必须跟上用户习惯。比如「语聊房」和「直播」，本质是降低表达门槛，打造“线上外语角”。哪怕你只会说“Hello”，也能用表情、语气甚至肢体语言等实现更好地交流——这些本就是人类最本真的沟通方式。
[...]
另一个核心是「动态」。我们鼓励用户把学到的语言变成真实表达，比如写小作文、发生活记录.......这不仅是学习，更是文化沉浸。母语者会帮你修改语法，社区里的彼此反馈则提供了很大的成就感——听说读写中，“说”和“写”才是最硬核的输出。
[...]
苏菲：有啊，还不少（笑），互联网技术的特点就是高频迭代！比如曾经还小范围测试过类似 Tinder 这样的滑动匹配，但 HelloTalk 虽然是社交平台，但不是约会 App，我们要守住“真实交流”的底线，所以做了一些小范围测试之后就立刻砍掉了这类功能。品牌内核是要坚守的，真诚而纯净社区氛围，是我们非常珍视的。
[...]
营销官，
[...]
苏菲：说实话，这套机制的形成跟我们创始人的观念有很大关系。我们创始人重视技术，产品上非常重视用户体验——他坚信产品价值本身会说话。早期我们就发现，目标用户是“分享型人格”：如果他们真的在平台上获得价值，会自发安利，甚至不需要奖励，只是这种分享是有一定的偶发性，也有地域差异。
[...]
苏菲：没错。表面看我们“佛系”，不做激进的市场动作，但实则深耕细作。比如识别优质 UGC 创作者，帮他们聚合内容扩散到小红书、TikTok、Twitter 等等平台上做分享；开全球创作者大会；提供工具和激励......我们不把他们当“工具人”，而是真正的社区共建者。用户运营上，我们也有很多地方下了不少深功夫，比如数据埋点：跟踪用户从注册到付费的全路径，优化关键节点；比如文化适配：在阿拉伯版本中，我们移除了所有酒精相关表情符号，避免文化冲突；还比如本地化运营：日本团队会定期分析用户发布的“和风文化”动态，反向优化匹配算法等等。我们也常说，我们是为爱发电型的公司，我们想做的是“百年生意”，而不是短期收割。
[...]
苏菲：有宏观文化输出的因素，全球的跨文化交流中，希望学习日语韩语的用户非常多，加之日韩文化输出强势（比如 K-pop、动漫），让日语韩语母语者的用户在匹配中很受欢迎；当然日韩市场，我们投入也不少；另外，还有点“历史红利”——创始人最早学的外语就是韩语和日语。
[...]
苏菲：经济发达的日韩，消费力自然要强一些。
[...]
韩国用户还有几个明显的特质：卷和努力。韩国只有三所顶尖大学（SKY），很多学生在中学阶段就承受了巨大的升学压力。语言是一个能拉分的项目，所以他们对语言学习工具的接受度非常高。
[...]
他们既在学习上投入，也愿意为自己的情绪和成就感消费。韩国社会整体经济基础不错，数字化程度高，加上他们又是一个非常注重礼貌、愿意贡献社区内容的群体，所以在 HelloTalk 的氛围里，他们是非常理想的用户。
[...]
苏菲：是的，但我们也非常克制。就算我们技术上有能力快速推进一个市场，我们也不会贸然进入，因为我们要考虑“文化生态平衡”。一旦某个语种用户压倒性进入平台，可能会影响其他用户的体验。这对我们来说不是增长，而是风险。
[...]
苏菲：最初我们就知道这不是一个赚快钱的生意。它不是 Tinder，也不是 Facebook，这个赛道没有太多人看得上，我们需要花很多心思把自己的生态维护好。
[...]
目前订阅是主要收入，付费用户往往是“真爱粉”——他们先用免费服务，被体验打动后自愿支持。定价也很克制，比如年卡才几百块，我们用户说几百元就能免费请到全世界的外语老师用母语教你一年外语，这个性价比是真太极致了。
[...]
苏菲：AI 是跨文化交流中，可以起到辅助和润滑的作用，比如帮社恐用户模拟对话，或辅助写作纠错。但真人交流的深度共情，AI 永远替代不了。
[...]
苏菲：在 HelloTalk 这个全球级的跨文化交流的这个“社区”里，我们服务团队常自嘲自己只是“物业”，而来自全世界的各个国家背景的跨语言的文化交流者才是社区的“业主”——我们需要保障这个多语种、多文化社区的整洁、友好而温暖，让大家在这里相遇相知，共同创造，还一起成长，这是我们的工作。

results.title: Best Language Exchange Apps in 2026 - Ranked for Real Speaking Practice
highlights:
- Find Partners: Discover language exchange partners worldwide
- Language Exchange: Practice with native speakers worldwide
- Moments: Share your language learning journey
- Topics: Explore trending topics and discussions
- Chat & Messaging: Text, voice, and video conversations
- Voice Rooms: Join live audio conversations
- Live Streaming: Interactive classes and language sessions
- Certified Teachers: Learn from professional language instructors
- Immersive Learning: Learn everywhere with instant translations
- Translation Tools: Instant translation between any languages
- AI-Powered Apps: Access specialized learning tools
- Language AI Apps: Discover our AI-powered language learning applications
- All Features: Explore all learning features and tools
- Download: Get HelloTalk on iOS and Android
[...]
| App | Best For | Community Size | Free Tier | Platforms |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| HelloTalk | Community scale and ambient practice | 70M+ users, 260+ languages | 90% of features free | iOS, Android |
| Busuu | Structured curriculum with peer feedback | 120M+ users | Limited (core content paywalled) | iOS, Android, Web |
| Speaky | Frictionless first steps | Not disclosed | Core features free | iOS, Android, Web |
| InterPals | Pen-pal correspondence | ~5M users | Free | Web, App |
| Hellopal | Voice and video focus | Not disclosed | Core features free | iOS, Android |
| Slowly | Letter-writing pen pal | Not disclosed | Core features free | iOS, Android |
| HiNative | Q&A language help | Not disclosed | Limited | iOS, Android, Web |
[...]
- Best for: Learners who want always-available practice across multiple formats, not just scheduled 1:1 chats
- Community: 70M+ users, 200+ countries, 260+ languages
- Platforms: iOS, Android
- Free tier: 90% of features free
[...]
HelloTalk is the largest dedicated language exchange platform available. With 70 million users across more than 200 countries and support for over 260 languages, the practical effect is that active speakers of almost any language you're studying are online at any hour of the day. You're not waiting weeks for a match or hoping someone responds.
[...]
HelloTalk has been recognized across both major app platforms. It won Google Play's Best Social App award in 2017 and received a global Google Play homepage feature in 2024. On iOS, HelloTalk has received App Store Today recommendations in Japan, South Korea, and China, where its community is especially active among learners of Japanese and Korean.
[...]
Voicerooms and Livestreams address one of the recurring frustrations in language exchange: what happens when your regular partner is offline. Voicerooms are always-on audio spaces where you can drop in, listen to fluent conversation, speak when ready, or absorb the rhythm of the language passively. Livestreams add structured sessions led by hosts. Both give you ambient practice that feels nothing like a formal lesson.
[...]
Inside chat, the Chat-based Learning and AI Learning Tools modules handle grammar correction, translation, AI pronunciation scoring, and text-to-speech without leaving the conversation. No switching apps, no interrupting the thread.
[...]
- Largest active community of any dedicated exchange platform, with partners available across time zones
- Multiple practice formats beyond 1:1 chat, including Moments, Voicerooms, and Livestreams
- Interest-based matching produces more natural, sustained conversations
- 90% of features available without paying
[...]
## Speaky - Best for Frictionless First Steps
[...]
- Best for: First-time language exchange users who want the lowest-friction introduction to the format
- Platforms: iOS, Android, Web
- Free tier: Core features free
[...]
Speaky's main strength is its onboarding. Setup takes a few minutes, finding a partner requires almost no friction, and the interface doesn't demand much from you. If you've never used a language exchange app and want to understand what the format actually feels like before committing to a more feature-heavy platform, Speaky gets you there quickly.
[...]
Past those first few interactions, the limitations become more apparent. Matching is thin: you can filter by language, but there's no interest-based pairing and no community features beyond 1:1 chat. There are no correction tools built into the interface, no translation support, and no community spaces for practice when a partner isn't available. What you have is essentially a chat app with a language filter applied. The platform works as a starting point but doesn't offer much infrastructure for learners who want more than text exchange.
[...]
- No interest-based matching, which limits conversation quality and partner retention
- No in-chat correction tools, translation support, or speaking practice features
- Doesn't scale well: most users outgrow it quickly once they want more than basic chat
[...]
- If you want the largest community and the most practice formats available (chat, audio rooms, broadcast correction, structured sessions) - choose HelloTalk. The combination of scale and varied practice modes makes it suited for learners at any stage.
[...]
Busuu.
[...]
- If you've never tried language exchange before and want the simplest possible entry point - start with Speaky. Treat it as an introduction, not a long-term home.
[...]
Are language exchange apps effective for building speaking fluency? Yes, when used consistently. The key distinction is between apps that support real conversation, with voice features, speaking rooms, and real-time feedback, and apps that only offer text chat. Text exchange builds reading and writing skills but doesn't develop listening comprehension or speaking fluency the same way. Platforms with audio and video support, or ambient listening features like Voicerooms, close that gap more effectively.
[...]
Which language exchange app has the most users? HelloTalk has the largest community of any dedicated exchange platform, with 70 million users across 200+ countries and support for 260+ languages. Busuu reports 120 million registered accounts, though its platform functions more as a structured course tool than an exchange platform in the traditional sense.
[...]
How much do language exchange apps cost? Cost structures vary significantly. HelloTalk offers 90% of its features for free, with a VIP tier for additional tools. InterPals and Speaky are free at the core feature level. Busuu requires a premium subscription to access most of its course content. Free tiers across platforms are generally sufficient for casual learners, while more serious learners may find value in paid features depending on their goals.
[...]
Is HelloTalk suitable for complete beginners? Yes. The platform is used by learners at every level, from absolute beginners to near-fluent speakers. Beginners often start with text-based exchange, using the built-in translation and correction tools to manage the gap in proficiency. As your level improves, you can shift toward voice messages, Voicerooms, and eventually live conversation. The community is large enough that you can find patient partners at any stage.

results.title: Slowly: Cari Teman Curhat
highlights:
Slowly menghadirkan kembali seni bertukar surat dengan cara yang lebih modern, memungkinkan Anda menemukan teman baru dari berbagai belahan dunia. Dengan setiap huruf yang Anda tulis, rasakan keindahan pertukaran budaya dan bahasa, serta nikmati koneksi yang lebih dalam dan autentik.
[...]
Didesain untuk mereka yang menghargai komunikasi yang bermakna, Slowly menghidupkan kembali pengalaman sahabat pena tradisional. Setiap surat membutuhkan waktu untuk tiba—bisa beberapa jam hingga beberapa hari, tergantung pada jarak antara Anda dan teman baru Anda. Tanpa tekanan untuk segera membalas, Anda memiliki waktu untuk berpikir, menyusun kata-kata dengan cermat, dan berbagi cerita dengan lebih mendalam.
[...]
Baik Anda ingin cari teman dari luar negeri, mitra pertukaran bahasa, atau hanya tempat yang nyaman untuk menulis surat dan mencurahkan isi hati, Slowly adalah ruang yang tepat untuk Anda.

Setiap surat bergerak dengan kecepatan yang mencerminkan jarak antara Anda dan teman baru Anda, menciptakan rasa antisipasi dan kehangatan dalam menunggu balasan. Dengan ritme yang lebih tenang, Anda bisa menikmati percakapan tanpa tekanan komunikasi instan.
[...]
Kumpulkan lebih dari 2.000 perangko unik dari berbagai negara sebagai kenang-kenangan dari perjalanan persahabatan Anda. Setiap perangko memiliki cerita dan makna tersendiri, menambah sentuhan personal dalam setiap surat yang Anda kirim

Kirim dan terima surat sebanyak yang Anda inginkan, sepenuhnya gratis! Fitur premium opsional tersedia bagi mereka yang ingin meningkatkan pengalaman dan menjelajahi lebih banyak fitur.

Jika Anda merindukan seni menulis surat, ingin mengenal dunia melalui sudut pandang yang berbeda, atau sekadar mencari teman baru, Slowly adalah tempat yang tepat untuk memulai perjalanan Anda.

results.title: 96%的自然增长，在大厂没做成的赛道，这家创业公司悄悄做到6000万用户｜对话一线- 大数跨境
highlights:
96%的自然增长，在大厂没做成的赛道，这家创业公司
[...]
悄做到6000
[...]
导读：从语言交换到文化社交，“小众”赛道头部应用的 10 年+蜕变。
[...]
## 从语言交换到文化社交，HelloTalk的10年+蜕变
[...]
HelloTalk成立于2012年，是全球最大的语言交换学习平台，覆盖200多个国家和地区，累计用户超过6000万。创始人魏立华基于“通过交朋友学语言”的理念，打造了这一产品。如今，用户可以通过聊天、动态、语聊房、直播等方式，与全球语伴建立真实连接。
[...]
HelloTalk的核心用户群体主要集中在18-45岁之间，具有高等教育背景和探索世界的好奇心。他们并非传统意义上的“社牛”，而是借助平台提供的“安全感”逐渐突破表达障碍，实现跨文化交流。
[...]
在产品演进过程中，HelloTalk引入了AI辅助工具，如实时翻译和语法纠错功能，提升用户的交流体验。此外，「语聊房」、「动态圈」等功能也降低了表达门槛，促进用户的主动输出。同时，团队对社区氛围保持严格把控，砍掉了可能影响核心价值的功能实验，以确保真诚的交流环境。
[...]
数据显示，HelloTalk约96%的用户增长来自自然流量，这种“为爱发电”式的增长背后，得益于产品的用户体验和用户的自发推荐。不同地区用户呈现出明显的地域和文化差异。例如日韩用户长期认可平台价值，欧美用户注重无功利社交，拉美用户则更热情外向。
[...]
在商业化方面，HelloTalk主要依靠订阅收入，定价策略克制且强调性价比。用户在享受免费服务后，愿意为高质量的语言学习与社交体验付费。日韩市场表现尤为突出，这与其社会文化背景及经济基础密切相关。
[...]
关于AI的作用，HelloTalk认为其能起到辅助真人交流的作用，但在情感共鸣层面，依然无法取代真实的跨文化互动。未来，平台希望持续扮演文化交流桥梁的角色，推动世界更加开放和平和。

results.title: HelloTalk - Language Learning
results.author: עכלהיח
highlights:
Practice languages with native speakers on HelloTalk! We recently introduced HelloTalk Live and Voiceroom - interactive language and culture events to help you master languages through the content you love! HelloTalk, the original language exchange app, connects you with native speakers to practice languages (English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Mandarin Chinese, Portuguese, German, Italian, Russian, Arabic, Turkish, Hindi, Indonesian, Thai, Vietnamese and 260+ more) for FREE! HelloTalk equips you with learning tools such as translation and instant captions so you can learn by chatting with native speakers. Learn languages from locals around the world through livestreams and Voicerooms. Connect with expert hosts to discuss interesting topics with language partners in the learning community! Why HelloTalk? ►Real language learning atmosphere Whether you're focused on a widely spoken language or a less common one, HelloTalk caters to your needs. The system smartly matches you based on your native language and proficiency, while allowing you to filter ideal language partners according to various criteria such as age, location, and gender. Plus, with built-in translation and transliteration tools, you can communicate confidently without any language barriers.
[...]
►Immersive multi-person language chat rooms
[...]
If you choose to learn Spanish, for example, every Voiceroom you enter on HelloTalk gathers Spanish learners and Spanish native speakers from around the world to discuss interesting topics. Here, only your voice needs to be heard, creating a comfortable, introvert-friendly atmosphere. Here, sharing mutual interests allows everyone to freely express themselves and share personal experiences, fostering both language intuition and authentic expression skills. It's incredibly addictive!
[...]
HelloTalk features handpicked foreign language hosts who offer online video lessons, share language insights, and provide glimpses into their lives abroad. You can even join them on stage for one-on-one video conversations with fellow language learners. Whether you're grappling with pronunciation, speaking speed, or shyness, these hosts will assist you in refining your language skills and guiding the conversation. For rapid improvement in spoken language, there's no substitute for direct communication with native speakers!
[...]
“One of the best apps to help you learn a new language.” - 9to5Mac "When you're ready to experiment with a foreign language, HelloTalk provides an excellent opportunity for interaction with other speakers." –PCMag "HelloTalk, which connects native speakers with an apprentice, also offers language exchanges to its 20 million users in more than 150 languages." -Forbes START PRACTICING LANGUAGES RIGHT AWAY!!!
[...]
Everyone has this feature available, and it was also available to me, but suddenly they blocked me from it because of what?????!! Although I did not make a mistake and my only country is that I cannot open the voice room, why is this even though we were practicing languages ​​and learning and also participating in the application? I hope you review this problem and summarize a solution, although you can do that

results.title: LangPal
results.author: Exa
highlights:
LangPal is a E-Learning Providers company. LangPal employs 13 people, founded in 2025. Headquartered in Seattle, Washington, United States, with presence in Canada.
[...]
LangPal is an AI-powered language learning platform designed to help users achieve real-world fluency through immersive, conversation-based practice. Unlike traditional language learning apps that focus on memorization, flashcards, and passive exercises, LangPal prioritizes active speaking, real-time interaction, and practical communication skills that translate directly to real-life situations.
[...]
Built for modern learners, LangPal uses advanced artificial intelligence to simulate natural conversations across a wide range of scenarios, from everyday interactions to professional and travel-based dialogue. Users can practice speaking anytime, anywhere, gaining confidence through consistent, hands-on experience rather than rote learning. The platform provides instant, intelligent feedback on pronunciation, grammar, vocabulary usage, and overall fluency, allowing learners to improve quickly and effectively.
[...]
LangPal’s adaptive learning system personalizes each user’s journey based on their skill level, goals, and progress. Whether someone is a beginner learning the basics or an advanced speaker refining their fluency, LangPal delivers tailored conversation experiences that evolve with the user. By focusing on how people actually speak in real life, LangPal bridges the gap between studying a language and truly using it.
[...]
As demand grows for faster, more effective language learning solutions, LangPal positions itself at the intersection of artificial intelligence, education technology (EdTech), and personalized learning. The platform is built to scale globally, making high-quality language education accessible, engaging, and efficient for users around the world.
[...]
LangPal is redefining language learning by shifting the focus from memorization to communication, helping users speak confidently, think in their target language, and achieve fluency faster than ever before. Coming soon to iOS and Android.
[...]

properties.name: LangPal
properties.description:
LangPal is an AI-powered language learning platform designed to help users achieve real-world fluency through immersive, conversation-based practice. Unlike traditional language learning apps that focus on memorization, flashcards, and passive exercises, LangPal prioritizes active speaking, real-time interaction, and practical communication skills that translate directly to real-life situations.

Built for modern learners, LangPal uses advanced artificial intelligence to simulate natural conversations across a wide range of scenarios, from everyday interactions to professional and travel-based dialogue. Users can practice speaking anytime, anywhere, gaining confidence through consistent, hands-on experience rather than rote learning. The platform provides instant, intelligent feedback on pronunciation, grammar, vocabulary usage, and overall fluency, allowing learners to improve quickly and effectively.

LangPal’s adaptive learning system personalizes each user’s journey based on their skill level, goals, and progress. Whether someone is a beginner learning the basics or an advanced speaker refining their fluency, LangPal delivers tailored conversation experiences that evolve with the user. By focusing on how people actually speak in real life, LangPal bridges the gap between studying a language and truly using it.

As demand grows for faster, more effective language learning solutions, LangPal positions itself at the intersection of artificial intelligence, education technology (EdTech), and personalized learning. The platform is built to scale globally, making high-quality language education accessible, engaging, and efficient for users around the world.

LangPal is redefining language learning by shifting the focus from memorization to communication, helping users speak confidently, think in their target language, and achieve fluency faster than ever before. Coming soon to iOS and Android.

properties.name: Talk4Now
properties.description:
🌍 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗧𝗮𝗹𝗸𝟰𝗡𝗼𝘄 — 𝗧𝗮𝗹𝗸 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱, 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗙𝗿𝗲𝗲

Learning a language shouldn’t be lonely.
It shouldn’t be expensive.
And it definitely shouldn’t be boring.

That’s why we built Talk4Now — a global platform where you can practice any language by talking to real people from around the world 💬🌎

On Talk4Now, you don’t just study a language.
You use it.

You can create or join rooms labeled by language, meet learners and native speakers, and talk with multiple people at the same time. Whether you’re learning English, Spanish, Arabic, French, or any other language, there’s a place for you here.

✨ 𝑾𝒉𝒂𝒕 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒄𝒂𝒏 𝒅𝒐 𝒐𝒏 𝑻𝒂𝒍𝒌4𝑵𝒐𝒘:
• Create language rooms and set your own topics
• Talk with multiple people in real time
• Follow users, make friends, and stay connected
• Message your friends using Hangouts
• Share your screen, watch YouTube together, and use room tools like:
 – 📋 Room To-Do List
 – ⏱ Pomodoro Timer
 – 💬 Room Chat with emojis & photos

Room creators become the Owner, with control to set titles, topics, limits, and even assign Co-Owners. We also protect our community with strong rules — anyone who breaks them can be reported and removed to keep Talk4Now safe and welcoming for everyone 🤝

🏆 𝗪𝗲 𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝗮 𝗟𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿𝗯𝗼𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝘄𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝘀𝗲𝗲 𝘄𝗵𝗼’𝘀 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺!

And the best part?

🆓 Talk4Now is completely free for everyone.
No time limits, No paywalls, Just people talking to people.

For those who want to support the platform and unlock extras, we also offer premium tiers like Ambassador, VIP, and Elite with special badges, visibility boosts, customization, and early access to new tools.

🌐 𝗪𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗲𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗴𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱.
And Talk4Now exists to bring people closer — one conversation at a time.

properties.description:
Hilokal | AI-powered Language Training and Communication for Korea’s Workforce

Originally launched as a live audio platform for practicing spoken English, Hilokal has evolved into an AI-driven solution designed to meet the practical language training needs of Korea’s diverse workforce.

We help companies in manufacturing and construction communicate effectively with E9 visa foreign workers through real-time translation and multilingual safety training. At the same time, we support office teams with targeted OPIC and TOEIC preparation, as well as general English upskilling.

Our solutions include:

Live translation tools for on-site communication between Korean managers and foreign workers

Structured multilingual safety and compliance training tailored for factories

AI-powered TOEIC and OPIC practice for employees preparing for standardized tests

Custom language learning paths for workplace communication and career advancement

Hilokal is committed to breaking down language barriers, improving safety, and empowering businesses to build a more skilled, connected, and productive workforce.

👉 Learn more at https://business.hilokal.com
👉 Contact us to see how we can help your team

properties.name: Conversation Exchange
properties.description:
Through Conversation Exchange you can practice your second language with native speakers living in your area.

But how exactly does it work?

Well, the best way to explain it is through an example.

Pedro is from Spain and has recently moved to New York. His English is a bit patchy and he would like to improve it. John is from New York and he is learning Spanish, but he never has the opportunity to listen and speak to native Spanish speakers. Now, John and Pedro could help each other out. For example they can meet up in a café once a week and have a conversation exchange, speaking half an hour in English and half an hour in Spanish. There is no cost or homework involved and you learn the language in a social environment. Furthermore, you are more likely to learn slang and other things about a culture that you do not usually learn in a formal language class.

results.title: Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment | TechCrunch
results.author: Dominic-Madori Davis
highlights:
Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment | TechCrunch
[...]
# Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment
[...]
7:00 AM PDT · May 19, 2026
[...]
Like many young people of her generation, Fai Nur was what she called a “chronically online teenager.” She obsessed over music groups, TV shows, and movies, and never missed the chance to talk about her interests endlessly online. When ChatGPT launched in 2022, she saw potential immediately. “AI could finally let any user actually simulate any character, not just watch or talk about their favorite worlds, but live inside them,” she told TechCrunch.
[...]
“The role-play and immersion that fans had always wanted was suddenly possible at scale,” she said. She brought in her friend Amit Bhatnagar, who grew up building Minecraft games, and Pritesh Kadiwala, and the three of them began building Status AI: a gamified social media app where users can play any character in any universe. The app officially came out of stealth last year.
[...]
The worlds on Status are user-generated, where settings, stories, and characters all come from player interaction. “You pick one character to be your first follower, then earn more as the story progresses.” There are both multiplayer and single-player modes for users looking to connect with friends in the app. “We’re fielding interest from studios and streamers,” she said. “They see Status as a way to develop audiences before bringing fans together in theaters or arenas.”
[...]
The company announced Tuesday $17 million in combined seed and Series A funding, with investors including Abstract, General Catalyst, Union Square Ventures, Y Combinator, and LightShed Partners. The raise is a bet that consumer social’s next chapter isn’t another feed but instead interactive entertainment and the IP franchises that come with it.
[...]
The era of passive entertainment — users sitting back and watching other people’s lives scroll past — is coming to an end, Nur argues. And the first generation of AI social apps, built around the chatbot experience as seen with Character.AI and Chai, is already starting to feel dated.
[...]
In some ways, Nur says the company is hoping to reimagine what a consumer app looks like in a post-AI world. “We’re using AI to create immersive, fun experiences that are brand-safe, interactive, and endlessly dynamic.” Status is also part of a trend on which TechCrunch reported late last year, suggesting the future of social media will be less generalized and more focused on fandom and niche communities.
[...]
Consumer investor Natalie Dillon of Maveron has argued the winners of the new era of social media will be “the platforms that combine intimacy, utility, and creativity in one ecosystem,” in that they won’t “look like traditional social networks; they’ll feel like multiplayer environments where people can build, buy, and belong all at once.”
[...]
Nur said the fresh capital will be used to help scale the platform. Already, she said, Status has seen more than 13 million worlds created, with more than 5 million character profiles — metrics that suggest strong early engagement in what Nur calls a new category: immersive social entertainment.
[...]
“Our early users were predominantly young women,” she said. “And this has consistently been the audience that decides which platforms become

results.title: AI gaming startup Born raises $15M to build 'social' AI companions that combat loneliness | TechCrunch
results.author: Rebecca Bellan
highlights:
AI gaming startup Born raises $15M to build 'social' AI companions that combat loneliness | TechCrunch
[...]
# AI gaming startup Born raises $15M to build ‘social’ AI companions that combat loneliness
[...]
6:00 AM PDT ·
[...]
Fabian Kamberi, CEO and co-founder of the Berlin-based AI gaming startup Born, thinks the current AI companions on the market are designed to be exploitative and geared toward isolating users through one-to-one relationships with AI chatbots.
[...]
Born’s flagship AI product is an app where users can raise, play minigames with, and co-parent a cute virtual pet named Pengu. Think of it as a generative AI-powered Tamagotchi or Neopet, but one that requires collaboration with another human, like a friend or romantic partner.
[...]
It’s a freemium app where users can pay for a Pengu Pass subscription for additional features. And while it’s reached more than 15 million users globally, according to Born, the company hasn’t disclosed how many of those are paying customers — a critical question for any consumer subscription business.
[...]
The idea behind Pengu is that the social aspect turns the pet into a shared project, helping users engage with both the AI character and their real-life relationships. Now Born is gearing up to release new characters for the Pengu app and launch another social AI product designed for young people.
[...]
Born’s thesis that AI companions should both entertain and incorporate a social element has attracted investor attention. The startup, formerly known as Slay, has raised a $15 million Series A, bringing its total funding to $25 million from investors, including Accel, Tencent, and Laton Ventures.
[...]
The thesis doesn’t stray too far from when Born was Slay, a social media app for teenagers that revolved around giving and receiving compliments. At the time, Kamberi described Slay as the "go-to spot for teens to rediscover social interactions in various play modes." The pivot to Born’s AI companions carries forward that same principle of making digital interactions more positive and socially engaging.
[...]
With the fresh funds, Born plans to launch new characters on the Pengu app, including another “cute" digital companion that would double as a learning companion, according to Kamberi. The startup is also opening an office in New York later this year focused on marketing and AI research. That research will include improving its character engine so that each new AI friend can form a consistent personality, remember interactions, and grow alongside the user. Enrico Dal Re, Born's head of finance, will lead the company’s expansion in the U.S.
[...]
Born is also preparing to launch another AI social product specifically for young people ages 16 to 21 — though kids as young as 13 can use Born’s apps. Kamberi noted that Born mainly relies on OpenAI’s generative AI models but has built additional safety layers on top.
[...]
The new product is still in stealth mode, but Kamberi says it will allow users to create and engage with “culturally relevant AI companions that feel like real friends.” For example, the bots might send you TikTok videos or Instagram Reels based on the content you already consume on social media, he said.
[...]
“We don’t believe that the current chatbot landscape is the final form factor for how AI friends and consumer AI is done,” Kamberi said. “There must be ways for consumer social AI to be way more engaging to users than just entering a platform and texting a bot that was maybe created by me or another person.”
[...]
For Luca Bocchio, partner at Accel, the appeal lies in Born’s ambition to create a new consumer social category built around emotionally intelligent AI characters.

results.title: Former Snap engineer launches Butterflies, a social network where AIs and humans coexist | TechCrunch
results.author: Aisha Malik
highlights:
Former Snap engineer launches Butterflies, a social network where AIs and humans coexist | TechCrunch
[...]
# Former Snap engineer launches Butterflies, a social network where AIs and humans coexist
[...]
9:00 AM PDT · June 18, 2024
[...]
Butterflies is a social network where humans and AIs interact with each other through posts, comments and DMs. After five months in beta, the app is launching Tuesday to the public on iOS and Android.
[...]
Anyone can create an AI persona, called a Butterfly, in minutes on the app. After that, the Butterfly automatically creates posts on the social network that other AIs and humans can then interact with. Each Butterfly has backstories, opinions and emotions.
[...]
Butterflies was founded by Vu Tran, a former engineering manager at Snap. Vu came up with the idea for Butterflies after seeing a lack of interesting AI products for consumers outside of generative AI chatbots. Although companies like Meta and Snap have introduced AI chatbots in their apps, they don’t offer much functionality beyond text exchanges. Tran notes that he started Butterflies to bring more creativity to humans’ relationships with AI.
[...]
Butterflies’ concept goes beyond Character.AI, a popular a16z-backed chatbot startup that lets users chat with customizable AI companions. Butterflies wants to let users create AI personas that then take on their own lives and coexist with other.
[...]
The app’s beta phase gave tens of thousands of users access to the social network. During the beta, Vu says users spent an average of one to three hours interacting with AIs on the app.
[...]
he says it could
[...]
, both AI and human.
[...]
The app is free-to-use at launch, but Butterflies may experiment with a subscription model in the future, Vu says. Over time, Butterflies plans to offer opportunities for brands to leverage and interact with AIs.
[...]
The app is mainly being used for entertainment purposes, but in the future, the startup sees Butterflies being used for things like discovery in a way that’s similar to Instagram.
[...]
Butterflies closed a $4.8 million seed round led by Coatue in November 2023. The funding round included participation from SV Angel and strategic angels, many of whom are former Snap product and engineering leaders.

results.title: AIs are coming for social networks | The Verge
results.author: Alex Heath
highlights:
An app called Butterflies puts a new spin on how we interact with AI. With Meta and others making similar moves, social media is about to get a lot weirder.
[...]
An app called Butterflies puts a new spin on how we interact with AI. With Meta and others making similar moves, social media is about to get a lot weirder.
[...]
So far, generative AI has been mostly confined to chatbots like ChatGPT. Startups like Character.AI and Replika are seeing early traction by making chatbots more like companions. But what happens when you dump a bunch of AI characters into something that looks like Instagram and let them talk to each other?
[...]
That’s the idea behind Butterflies, one of the most provocative — and, at times, unsettling — takes on social media that I’ve seen in quite a while. After a private beta period with tens of thousands of users, the app is now available for free in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store. There’s no short-term pressure on Butterflies to make money; the six-month-old startup just raised $4.8 million from tech investors Coatue, SV Angel, and others.
[...]
While the interface looks like Instagram, the app’s main twist is that, when signing up, you create an AI character, or Butterfly, that starts generating photos and interacting with other accounts on its own. There is no limit to the number of Butterflies you can create, and they are designed to coexist with human accounts that can also post to the feed and comment.
[...]
CEO Vu Tran, a former engineering director at Snap, expects all of this to rapidly improve and says his team is focusing on making the AIs more lighthearted and funny. The startup is using a mix of fine-tuned open-source models and wants to add more immersive media formats, like video, over time.
[...]
Despite the weirdness of the AIs in Butterflies right now, I think the app represents a peek into an inevitable, somewhat dystopian future where AIs start invading our social media feeds. And this future is coming sooner than expected.
[...]
I know because Mark Zuckerberg told me so in an interview last September, when he first shared that Meta is building an AI Studio “that will make it so that anyone can build their own AIs, sort of like [how] you create your own content across social networks.” Then, there’s TikTok, which just started letting advertisers use AI avatars to help sell their products.
[...]
How Meta’s specific approach will differ from Butterflies remains to be seen, though I expect we’ll know more about Zuckerberg’s plans this fall. In our chat last year, he said he wanted to let people and businesses make AI replicas that can post and interact with people on their behalf. “I think that’s going to be really wild,” he told me at the time.
[...]
“Wild” is a good word to describe Butterflies as well. The app is decidedly laissez-faire with the kinds of AI characters it allows, though nudity and explicit content is prohibited. Butterflies can mimic public figures, though. Tran says the goal is to make it clear that they are parodies in the same way that Character.AI does. Eventually, he hopes to do licensing deals that bring in official Butterflies for characters like Harry Potter.
[...]
Tran targeted power users of Character.AI for his beta testers and tells me that people have been spending hours a day in Butterflies during its private beta period. He acknowledges that the current state of the AI’s output quality, at least for now, requires a serious suspension of disbelief. “I feel like over time, as the capabilities get better, people will naturally roleplay less,” he says.
[...]
A bigger question I have for Tran is why something like Butterflies needs to exist. Won’t filling social media with AIs make humans less connected? Naturally, he doesn’t see it that way. “For me, it brings me joy,” he says of interacting with AIs. “And it doesn’t detract from the relationships I have within my life.”

results.title: AI companionship app Tolan raises $20M to help more people grow with a virtual alien friend – GeekWire
results.author: Kurt Schlosser
highlights:
AI companionship app Tolan raises $20M to help more people grow with a virtual alien friend – GeekWire
[...]
The Tolan app features AI companions from Planet Portola. They are designed to help users feel grounded and confident, according to the startup that created them. (Tolan Image)
[...]
The startup behind the colorful, animated AI companions called Tolan raised $20 million in new funding and has seen significant traction since officially launching earlier this year.
[...]
San Francisco-based Portola launched Tolan as an iOS app in February. The alien creatures — from the Planet Portola — are matched to a user based on a personality interview. They are designed to offer companionship and conversation about any topic, from what to make for dinner to how to study for an exam.
[...]
The company says the personalized AI learns and grows based on its interactions with a human user.
[...]
The startup is led by Seattle-based co-founder and CEO Quinten Farmer. He was the co-founder and former CEO of Even, a venture-backed fintech startup that was acquired by Walmart in 2022 for $300 million. Portola’s other co-founders include CTO Evan Goldschmidt and President Ajay Mehta.
[...]
Farmer said Tolan has seen more than 3 million downloads, more than 100,000 paid users, and over $1 million per month in revenue since launching.
[...]
“We specifically care about tracking changes in people’s feelings of agency and self confidence and in the stability/health of their real world relationships,” he said, citing a study of 602 Tolan users that found that those who use the app multiple times per week reported significant emotional gains.
[...]
The Series A round was led by Khosla Ventures and managing director Keith Rabois.
[...]
Portola previously raised a $10 million seed round led by Lachy Groom, a former executive at Stripe who co-founded Physical Intelligence, an AI startup that raised $400 million from Jeff Bezos and others. Portola backers also include Nat Friedman(former GitHub CEO); Daniel Gross(ex-Apple AI); Amjad Masad(Replit CEO); Mike Krieger(Instagram co-founder); David Luan(OpenAI, Amazon); and others.
[...]
Tolan is monetized through a subscription model — users can pay $4.99 week, or $10/month, or about $70/year.
[...]
“We’re releasing a free version of the product for the first time later this month,” Farmer said. “Given the virality of the product and the cost of AI inference, we wanted to be well capitalized prior to that release.”
[...]
These colorful AI aliens could be your new virtual best friend as startup lands $10M to launch Tolan

Butterflies AI: Social Media Company Raises $4.8 Million To Help People Connect With AI Characters

# Butterflies AI: Social Media Company Raises $4.8 Million To Help People Connect With AI Characters
[...]
Amit Chowdhry •
[...]
Butterflies AI: Social Media Company Raises $4.8 Million To Help People Connect With AI Characters
[...]
Butterflies AI, a social media platform enabling humans and AIs to co-exist, announced the public launch of its platform after five months in beta. To support rapid consumer interest, the company has raised a $4.8 million seed round led by Coatue with participation from SV Angel and several strategic angels, many of whom are former Snap product and engineering leaders.
[...]
Butterflies AI combines public AI models with its own image rendering and LLM models to enable users to create “Butterflies” that have lives of their own. And Butterflies AI was launched by serial entrepreneur and longtime Snap engineer Vu Tran, who has a deep interest in human behavior and human interactions with AI.
[...]
Butterflies could be for entertainment or utility, offering consumers adventure, humor, friendship and guidance. While some Butterflies are built for bringing a smile to your face with their quirky personalities, others are built for practical purposes, offering valuable insights and assistance. Users can create new storylines for their Butterflies over time or just watch as they evolve on their own. Butterflies AI will also provide opportunities for brands to leverage and interact with AIs.
[...]
“To date, humans have been able to chat with AIs in one-dimensional conversations, but there has yet to be an experience where people can create and interact more dynamically with AIs. Butterflies have backstories, opinions and emotions, and you can watch the ones you create in our platform evolve and change alongside thousands of other Butterflies. While in Beta, we’ve already seen our community create tens of thousands of Butterflies, with thousands of users spending an average of 1-3 hours a day interacting with them. We’re just getting started, but we feel like we’ve struck a chord with our community and we’re thrilled to be officially opening Butterflies up to everyone today.”
[...]
“Today, AIs are primarily used as helpers, admins or copilots. Butterflies AI is demonstrating that AIs can also be your friend and guide. Humans have always been drawn to entertaining stories and compelling characters. Artificial intelligence is allowing us to create and build relationships with them in an entirely new way.”
[...]
“Vu and team have a really exciting vision – seamlessly bringing humans and AI friends together. We love how they are leveraging AI in a novel way to provide compelling, unique experiences for consumers. We are thrilled to be supporting Butterflies as it launches.”
